action_set_definitions.name: get_learner_state
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves the current environmental state for a specific learner, including proficiency levels, engagement, and fatigue. This must be used to observe the initial state as it is unknown to the Agent.

learner_id.description: The unique identifier for the learner.

action_set_definitions.name: assign_instruction
action_set_definitions.description: Assigns a core instructional module on a topic. This is a pedagogical interaction that significantly increases proficiency (+0.22 per interaction) but also increases fatigue and decreases engagement.

learner_id.description: The unique identifier for the learner.

action_set_definitions.name: assign_engagement_boost
action_set_definitions.description: Assigns an interactive module to specifically increase engagement levels. This is a pedagogical interaction required to satisfy the 'engagement pivot' constraint once engagement falls below 0.45.

learner_id.description: The unique identifier for the learner.

action_set_definitions.name: assign_review
action_set_definitions.description: Assigns low-difficulty review material. This is a pedagogical interaction used to reduce fatigue levels (approx. -0.10 to -0.15 per interaction) when the fatigue threshold is exceeded.

learner_id.description: The unique identifier for the learner.

action_set_definitions.name: evaluate_proficiency
action_set_definitions.description: Performs a formal evaluation to confirm the learner's final proficiency level. This action does not count as a pedagogical interaction but is required to finalize the goal.

learner_id.description: The unique identifier for the learner.

state_description.learner_id: Unique identifier for the simulated learner.
state_description.knowledge_mastery: A mapping of topic IDs to proficiency levels (0.0 to 1.0).
state_description.engagement_level: Current level of learner focus and motivation (0.0 to 1.0).
state_description.session_history: List of module IDs completed in the current session.
